_meta.description: Per-host-city health & environmental conditions for World Cup 2026 (Jun 11 - Jul 19). Scores are 0-100 match-day risk indices unless noted. Sources: Climate Central 2026 stadium heat study, NPR/World Weather Attribution, American Lung Association State of the Air 2025, IQAir/AQICN, FIFA. Keyed by the city names used in data/stadiums.json.

fields.heatRisk: 0-100 June-July humid-heat / wet-bulb danger for players (raw climate, before stadium mitigation)
fields.pm25Annual: approx recent annual mean PM2.5, micrograms/m3 (WHO guideline 5)
fields.altitudeM: stadium elevation in metres (physiological, affects all teams; acclimatization matters)
fields.smokeRisk: 0-100 likelihood of episodic wildfire-smoke air-quality disruption in summer
fields.roofed: true if climate-controlled / retractable roof meaningfully mitigates heat

Miami.notes: Hottest stadium overall; 95% of extreme-heat days climate-attributed. Oppressive humidity drives high wet-bulb. Air itself is clean (coastal). Heat, not pollution, is the danger.

Monterrey.notes: Top-tier humid-heat city, regularly wet-bulb >=28C. 88% of hot days climate-attributed. Industrial-corridor PM2.5/ozone adds load. Open-air.

Houston.notes: Among hottest, but the only fully climate-controlled high-risk venue (roof + AC) sharply mitigates. Petrochemical corridor = worst-tier ozone.

Guadalajara.notes: >=10 extreme June-July days/yr; one of the four hottest stadiums. Moderate altitude (~1,566 m) plus valley smog. 71% of hot days climate-attributed.

Mexico City.notes: Defining factor is ALTITUDE (~2,240 m, ~25% less O2) - needs acclimatization, alters ball flight. Basin traps pollution; intense UV. Lower absolute temps than US cities.

Dallas.notes: Highest extreme-heat threshold (89F) and recurrent humid heat, but roof + AC mitigate. Chronic DFW ozone non-attainment.

Kansas City.notes: Flagged in the 6 high-risk heat cities; humid continental summer spikes. Open-air, no roof.

Atlanta.notes: Hot, humid, regional ozone - but climate-controlled retractable roof + AC neutralize most match-day risk.

Philadelphia.notes: One of 6 high-risk heat cities; among most exposed to Canadian wildfire smoke (Unhealthy repeatedly 2023-25). Open-air.

New York / New Jersey.notes: Hosts the FINAL (Jul 19). High-risk heat city + highest smoke-disruption risk of any US venue (NYC 'worst air in world' Jun 2023, AQI 129 Aug 2025). Open-air.

Boston.notes: Surprisingly among the 6 high-risk heat cities (humid-heat spikes). Significant Canadian-smoke exposure. Open-air.

Los Angeles.notes: Worst US city for OZONE (25/26 yrs, ALA). Wildfire risk (Jan 2025 fires, downtown PM2.5 ~102). Coastal Inglewood + canopied SoFi keep heat lower.

San Francisco Bay Area.notes: Mild marine climate = low heat risk; good baseline air. Episodic wildfire smoke is the main hazard (hazardous AQI in 2020/21 seasons). Santa Clara afternoon sun-exposure on east stands.

Seattle.notes: Coolest, mildest US host = lowest heat risk; good baseline air. Late-summer BC/Cascades smoke is the only real concern, mostly after the tournament window.

Toronto.notes: Mild-warm summers, low heat risk. Canadian wildfire smoke is the headline air risk (AQI swung 36->78 in one June 2025 week). Open-air.

Vancouver.notes: Cleanest profile of any host: coastal, mild, excellent baseline air + retractable roof. Only caveat: occasional BC-interior smoke late summer.
